titleEn: Australian vegetable farm puts 2 weeding technologies to the test
titleZh: 澳大利亚蔬菜农场实测两种除草技术
summaryZh: 澳大利亚一家百年有机农场在蔬菜种植中测试了两种前沿除草技术：丹麦Agrointelli公司的自主作业平台Robotti和激光除草设备。该试验旨在解决有机农业中杂草控制这一核心难题，展示了自动化与精准技术在果蔬种植中的实际应用潜力，为智慧农业在种植业中的落地提供了参考案例。
featuredReason: 该新闻展示了自动化除草技术在有机蔬菜种植中的实际测试，虽然直接针对种植业，但其涉及的自主机器人、精准作业等技术与智慧畜牧中的自动化饲喂、巡检等场景高度相通，值得畜牧从业者借鉴跨界技术思路。
contentFull: Australian vegetable farm puts 2 weeding technologies to the test 24-06 | Crop solutions | ArticleLinkedInFacebookXWhatsAppPrintThe first machine to arrive at Busch Organics was the Robotti, an autonomous implement carrier developed by Danish company Agrointelli. Photo: Busch OrganicsThe Mitchell River Flats near Bairnsdale in Gippsland, Australia, are not the first place you might expect to find agricultural robotics at work. But on the organic farm that Kane Busch’s great-grandfather established more than a century ago, the family has trialed technology that could reshape how organic vegetables are grown. Busch Organics spans more than 300 acres (121 hectares) of river flat country, 13 kilometres west of Bairnsdale at Hillside. translationZh:

澳大利亚吉普斯兰拜恩斯代尔附近的米切尔河平原，可能不是你最先想到能看到农业机器人作业的地方。但在凯恩·布施的曾祖父一个多世纪前建立的有机农场上，这个家族试验了一项可能重塑有机蔬菜种植方式的技术。布施有机农场占地超过300英亩（121公顷），位于拜恩斯代尔以西13公里的希尔斯德河平原地区。获得有机认证意味着每一处
author: René Groeneveld
contentHtml: Manual weeding costs Australian organic grower Busch Organics a significant amount of time and money. Field trials with autonomous hoeing and laser weeding have brought the company closer to a possible solution.
